arguments.prompt:
Review all MSBuild project files (.csproj, .props, .targets, Directory.Build.props, Directory.Build.targets, Directory.Packages.props) in this .NET solution for anti-patterns, duplication, and modernization opportunities. The solution is at /tmp/sv-209de734b1f74eefb8116e63a2ccefea/ContosoApp.sln with source in src/ and tests in test/.

Focus on:
1. Duplicated properties across projects (TargetFramework, LangVersion, Nullable, etc.)
2. Scattered package versions that should use Central Package Management
3. Missing or underutilized Directory.Build.props/targets
4. SDK-style modernization opportunities
5. Anti-patterns (hardcoded paths, restated defaults, Exec instead of built-in tasks, etc.)
6. Any other MSBuild best practices violations

Provide a comprehensive report with specific findings and actionable recommendations. Show the current state (BAD) and recommended state (GOOD) for each finding.

# 🔍 MSBuild Project Files Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Overview

| File | SDK | Projects Referenced |
|------|-----|---------------------|
| `src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | — |
| `src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | Core |
| `src/ContosoApp.Api/ContosoApp.Api.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web | Core, Services |
| `test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | Core |
| `test/ContosoApp.Api.Tests/ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj` | Microsoft.NET.Sdk | Api |

**Missing infrastructure files:** No `Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, or `Directory.Packages.props` exist.

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely to cause issues)

### 1. Package Version Inconsistency — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced at **different versions** across projects, which can lead to diamond dependency conflicts at runtime:

| Project | Version |
|---------|---------|
| ContosoApp.Core | **8.0.0** |
| ContosoApp.Services | **8.0.1** |
| ContosoApp.Api | **8.0.0** |

**Risk:** Runtime `MissingMethodException` or `FileLoadException` when the resolved version doesn't match what a library was compiled against.

---

### 2. Test Package Version Skew

Test infrastructure packages are at different versions across the two test projects:

| Package | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

**Risk:** Inconsistent test behavior, harder to diagnose failures, unnecessary maintenance burden.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-patterns that should be fixed)

### 3. Massive Property Duplication — No `Directory.Build.props`

**All 5 projects** repeat the exact same properties:
```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **7 properties × 5 projects = 35 duplicated lines** that must be updated in lockstep.

**BAD** (current state in every `.csproj`):
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
  <Company>Contoso</Company>
  <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**GOOD** — Create a `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` only declares what's unique to it.

---

### 4. Scattered Package Versions — No Central Package Management (CPM)

Package versions are specified inline in each `.csproj`. With 5 projects and 8 distinct packages, version drift has already occurred (see Finding #1 and #2).

**BAD** (current):
```xml
<!-- In each .csproj -->
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
```

**GOOD** — Create `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Production -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then in each `.csproj`, remove the `Version` attribute:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
```

---

### 5. Duplicated Custom Target — `PrintBuildInfo`

The exact same target is copy-pasted in 3 source projects (`Core`, `Services`, `Api`):

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**BAD**: Repeated 3 times across files — if you want to change the message format, you must edit 3 files.

**GOOD** — Move to `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

---

### 6. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Property

All 3 source projects set `DocumentationFile` identically:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

**BAD**: Repeated in an extra `<PropertyGroup>` in each source project.

**GOOD** — Add to `Directory.Build.props` with a condition so test projects are excluded:
```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsPackable)' != 'false'">
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Note: `GenerateDocumentationFile` is the modern equivalent — it sets the output path automatically. The explicit `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml` pattern is restating the SDK default.

---

### 7. Custom Target Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` (Incremental Build Issue)

The `PrintBuildInfo` target has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, meaning it will **always execute** even during no-op builds. For a simple `<Message>` task this is low-cost, but it's still a best-practice violation that could mask genuinely skipped builds.

**BAD**:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
```

**GOOD** (if this target should always run, make it explicit):
```xml
<!-- This target intentionally always runs (informational only) -->
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
```

Or if you want proper incrementality:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)PrintBuildInfo.marker">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  <Touch Files="$(IntermediateOutputPath)PrintBuildInfo.marker" AlwaysCreate="true" />
</Target>
```

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements for readability/maintainability)

### 8. Test Projects Should Share Common Test Infrastructure

Both test projects repeat the same 4 testing packages. Using a `test/Directory.Build.props` would consolidate this:

**GOOD** — Create `test/Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Import parent Directory.Build.props -->
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <PropertyGroup>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each test `.csproj` only needs its `<ProjectReference>`.

---

### 9. Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` Blocks in Same File

`ContosoApp.Core.csproj`, `Services.csproj`, and `Api.csproj` all have a second `<PropertyGroup>` solely for `DocumentationFile`. This should be consolidated into the first `<PropertyGroup>`:

**BAD**:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  ...
</PropertyGroup>
...
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**GOOD**: Single property group (or better yet, centralize to `Directory.Build.props`).

---

### 10. `xunit.runner.visualstudio` Missing `PrivateAssets`

The xUnit runner package is a development-only dependency and should not flow to downstream consumers:

**BAD**:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
```

**GOOD**:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
```

---

## 🟢 Positive (Things done well)

- ✅ All projects are already **SDK-style** — no legacy migration needed
- ✅ `Nullable` is enabled across all projects (modern C# best practice)
- ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` enforced consistently
- ✅ `ImplicitUsings` enabled (less boilerplate)
- ✅ Test projects correctly set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>`
- ✅ Consistent `net8.0` targeting across all projects
- ✅ Clean project references with no circular dependencies

---

## 📋 Recommended Action Plan

| Priority | Action | Files to Create/Modify |
|----------|--------|----------------------|
| 1 | Create `Directory.Build.props` at repo root | New file |
| 2 | Create `Directory.Packages.props` for CPM | New file |
| 3 | Create `Directory.Build.targets` for shared targets | New file |
| 4 | Create `test/Directory.Build.props` for test infra | New file |
| 5 | Strip duplicated properties from all 5 `.csproj` files | All `.csproj` |
| 6 | Remove `Version=` from all `PackageReference` items | All `.csproj` |
| 7 | Add `PrivateAssets="all"` to test runner package | Test `.csproj` files |

### After cleanup, projects would look like:

**`src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** (from 26 → 10 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**`test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** (from 25 → 9 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

This reduces the total MSBuild XML across the solution from **~130 lines to ~50 lines**, with a single source of truth for all shared configuration.

<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

  <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
  <Company>Contoso</Company>
  <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**GOOD** — Create a `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` only declares what's unique to it.

**GOOD** — Create `Directory.Packages.props` at the repo root:

  </PropertyGroup>

    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then in each `.csproj`, remove the `Version` attribute:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
```

### 5. Duplicated Custom Target — `PrintBuildInfo`

The exact same target is copy-pasted in 3 source projects (`Core`, `Services`, `Api`):

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**GOOD** — Move to `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root:
```xml

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

All 3 source projects set `DocumentationFile` identically:
```xml

```

**BAD**: Repeated in an extra `<PropertyGroup>` in each source project.

**GOOD** — Add to `Directory.Build.props` with a condition so test projects are excluded:
```xml

</PropertyGroup>
```

**GOOD** (if this target should always run, make it explicit):

```

        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)PrintBuildInfo.marker">

  <Touch Files="$(IntermediateOutputPath)PrintBuildInfo.marker" AlwaysCreate="true" />
